subject: Making taxes simple & fair
body:
| | |

| |
---
|
---
| **_Making Taxes Simple & Fair_** Thirty-one years ago: That’s the last time our country came together to reform federal taxes. It was 1986, Ronald Reagan was president, and gadgets like cassette tapes, film cameras, and two-pound cell phones were still relevant parts of our lives (not yet replaced with the smartphone in your pocket).

Lots of things have changed since 1986, but our tax code isn’t one of them. While Americans have innovated and launched new businesses, government has been a roadblock, claiming more of what we earn. Our burdensome, outdated tax code has forced large businesses to send jobs and money overseas, and stifled the growth of the engine of our country’s economy – small businesses.

Under the leadership of **President Donald Trump** and our majorities in **Congress** , Republicans are working hard to grow our economy and put more money in our pockets through federal tax reform. With a proposal passed through the House and currently being considered tonight by the Senate, middle-class Hoosiers will receive a big tax cut, while the process of completing your taxes will become much simpler.

In Indiana, we know a thing or two about what happens when you cut taxes and eliminate burdensome business regulations. When government gets out of the way, job creators get to work – with more Hoosiers working now than ever before and an unprecedented number of job commitments coming to Indiana in the near future.

As Hoosier Republicans, we can be thankful for **our entire Republican House delegation** , who all voted in favor of tax reform. They stood with Hoosiers, and voted to make our taxes fairer, simpler and lower. **Sen. Todd Young** has expressed support for tax reform, but with a vote in the Senate slated to come soon, it remains to be seen whether Senator Joe Donnelly will stand with Hoosiers, or whether he'll vote with Chuck Schumer and his Democrat party bosses -- like he frequently does -- in an attempt to obstruct important reforms that are long overdue in Washington.

Let’s take the Indiana way to Washington, and let’s reform taxes to jumpstart the American economy!

-Kyle Hupfer

_Photo: Chairman Hupfer addresses a crowd of 1,000+ Hoosiers for our First Annual Team Holcomb Fall Dinner, which brought together Republicans from across the state to honor our veterans and active duty military. _

1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec67029bcf68980495aa6e250223f81ca1924bc4732b0169e25d7872a1732850f76971365c53ae192f861490cc987d551bfc77
Sessions says he supports new legislation to strengthen DEA enforcement

- The Washington Post

This week, Attorney General Jeff Sessions announced new steps in the Trump Administration’s fight against the opioid epidemic. “Today we are facing the deadliest drug crisis in American history,” said the Attorney General. The Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) will allocate $12 million in grants to local law enforcement officials and establish a new field division in Louisville to fight regional trafficking. .

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec670204324410c73cf02828fc6e9d1493d039a6c3b2a3fe445320ba72d2b7148b84fdba0c32993d202fd9021515680d7dc33e
Trump deregulation already saves $378 million, billions next year

- Washington Examiner

Even before the Administration’s anti-regulation push comes into full force next year, the President has saved taxpayers hundreds of millions of dollars from streamlining and other reforms thus far. “Simply cutting red tape and putting Obama-era regulations on hold have already saved $378 million since President Trump took office,” reports Paul Bedard, citing an American Action Forum report. “That savings is expected to jump into the billions next year when the administration’s anti-regulation campaign hits full stride.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec6702bdf889287a24ee5964afdca802875f68595b786504e448ffc99c1caa809cb51fde9e7828de0d925b0d7fcfa0b7cd6a0c
Abortion rate In U.S. reaches new low

- The Washington Times

The abortion rate in America has fallen 22 percent since 2005, reaching a new historic low, according to data released by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention last week.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec670287d1db75cc713e682c49145ce69abfbdb6a416c32720bbbb582e1ab244c2884460477a463a1d790281682655e4dd0b33
GOP tax reform will be a boon to African-American job creators

- Washington Examiner

Diante Johnson, president of the Black Conservative Foundation, writes that historic tax cuts are especially welcome news for the African-American entrepreneurial community. “African-Americans were better off in 2000 than they were just last year, according to the latest Census data,” Johnson writes, citing dropping median incomes, stagnant wages, and “alarmingly high” unemployment. Minority entrepreneurs own 29 percent of U.S. businesses, and lower taxes will allow them to expand opportunity in their communities significantly.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec6702b33a4c6ee22916c29be60f405901160ee707d18e56057749c32e4983e6501ba151090633eaa433ddce874e8159c36a2f
Trump ratchets up pressure on China with rare trade action

- Politico

The President launched a pair of investigations this week aimed at thwarting Chinese aluminum dumping in the United States. This action could result in steep duties being imposed on aluminum sheet imports valued at upwards of $600 million. President Trump "made a promise to . . . be more enforcement-minded than our predecessors,” Secretary of Commerce Wilbur Ross said. “We intend to make good on that promise.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec6702eb0340974601d98684d4b2f332074e25574ae52ddc38c69520e7ce5632cca440e2e27e7a1b5d45b27affe7ef856f0c96
People are noticing something very different about the Trump’s Christmas card vs. Obama’s

- The Daily Caller

Presidents each put a personal touch on how the White House celebrates the Christmas season. One small but important change Americans may notice this year: The Obama family’s holiday card last year made zero references to “Christmas.” The new First Family decided “Merry Christmas” should make a comeback.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec670269b19f567b841758ce37cbb9be8073b1ea603eb5beedbc5f502519f23df79877c83c71929cabb5982cdb26fe9c57d667
More U.S. shoppers than expected kick off holiday spending season

- Reuters

“More than 174 million U.S. shoppers made purchases over Thanksgiving weekend and Cyber Monday,” reports Richa Naidu, beating expectations and “signaling a strong start to the holiday quarter.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec67020681bcf4b0fc72c8c5f4f15ee3efa99c0ee23633071ffe5a77c7237fd7bfce73e7716141e46e639e82e423deeea11c08
TSA wait times under control during record-breaking Thanksgiving travel week

- The Hill

The Transportation Security Administration screened more than 21.6 million people last week – its busiest Thanksgiving week ever. Despite the crowds, about 98 percent of passengers waited less than 20 minutes in line at security checkpoints.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec6702fc9c23530cb2e4bee7641feb680ab5b70e85aa5d09e55e9d0cd38072347f1768ea370040fe7d70a9f78256ed5ecb2f19
Sec. Of Agriculture Sonny Perdue: GOP reforms ease burden on truckers and farmers, eliminate ‘distasteful’ death tax

- Breitbart

Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue says in an interview that the Republican tax cut plan will be especially beneficial for the transportation industry. “Many of these are small businesses, some mom-and-pop team [truck] drivers across the country,” he said.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec670254dbdf0243ff377e027d61855d555e5b130261c43ce584899cae1a3b693a09777785655ec4013d60b2aedddcedf0db0a
Harsanyi: This is why we can’t trust factcheckers, part infinity

- The Federalist

During a speech last week, Vice President Mike Pence made an innocuous comment that was obviously true. The Washington Post’s fact-checking team agreed it was true. But that didn’t stop them from awarding his claim a “three Pinnochios” condemnation.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec6702656a3fe62b0454b60289572de34e234378c333a2f0dc5a4707d25388ec54cbdea6f99597d70b91b9b84f3d35b7a98705
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5c441bb5e6ec670232fda38c2df5d4fb7ca8abc3658fbf04cfb61e5cd9efd134c8972ad280bd7fc39bbc29c752a36c072ece113b700fd9ab
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4673285284253fa105ac182c888cf11981b0d6a88186bd772f9ea9929fb781d72c3c3142b437f399916ab1f01c6d8bbe9

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Why I voted against Mitch McConnell's Tax Bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Peter, as long as I've been in the Senate, I've focused on working hard and reaching across the aisle to deliver results for Hoosier families and small businesses. |
---

| | **Breaking: McConnell Tax Bill Passes Senate**
**[I voted against it, which means the attacks are coming. Pitch in>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4eb1/768423100/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?refcode=20171202nd&amount=10")** Peter, as long as I've been in the Senate, **I 've focused on working hard and reaching across the aisle to deliver results for Hoosier families and small businesses**. That starts with a better tax code that leaves more money in the pockets of the Hoosiers who leave for work in the dark and come home in the dark to provide for their families. It means making it easier for businesses to create more good-paying jobs in America, and rewarding them for keeping those jobs here at home.

**I 've tried to work with everyone I can during our current national conversation about our tax code. During the last few months, I've even spoken with the President multiple times about ideas that both parties agree would move us _forward_.** But Mitch McConnell and his special interest allies crafted this tax bill in a partisan process and rushed it to the floor with little debate. It's a handout to the richest Americans who don't need more help and the biggest corporations, many of which have shipped their money and jobs to other countries.

**I'm proud to have voted against it because we need to start over and _work together_ to write a tax bill that's focused on common sense reform for the American middle class families who need it most.**

The special interests who stand to gain from this bill have already spent millions of dollars to get me to betray my constituents. Now they'll spend tens of millions more trying to replace me with someone who puts them first, not Hoosiers. **President Trump even promised to come to Indiana and "campaign against [me] like you wouldn't believe" if I didn't go along with them here.**

**Can you chip in to my Emergency Response Fund to show Mitch McConnell we won't be stuck with a tax bill for special interest giveaways?** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [Express Donate $5](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4eb0/768423100/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202nd&amount=5")
---
[Express Donate $10](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4eb3/768423100/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202nd&amount=10")
[Express Donate $25](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4eb2/768423100/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202nd&amount=25")
[Express Donate $50](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4ead/768423100/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202nd&amount=50")
[Donate another amount >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4eac/768423100/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?refcode=20171202nd")
Why is the McConnell tax bill wrong for Indiana? The list of reasons is long, but here are a few.
* Middle class Hoosier families deserve a tax cut, but this bill actually **RAISES taxes on a majority of families** earning less than $75,000 a year, including nearly one million Hoosier families. The cuts for most working families are temporary -- but the massive breaks for the biggest corporations are now permanent. Even worse, there's nothing here to encourage them to invest in American jobs, or penalize them for shipping jobs to foreign countries.

* This isn't just a bad tax bill -- it's a bad health care bill as well, especially for older Hoosiers. There's another attempt to dismantle Americans' health care system, one that would spike premiums and take away health insurance from enough Americans to equal Indiana's population twice over. And it **cuts Medicare by tens of billions of dollars at a minimum every single year.** Congress can't just give away older Hoosiers' hard-earned health care for another massive tax break to corporate America.

* Last, but not least, this bill is set to **add over _one trillion dollars_ \-- with a T! -- to our debt** over the next ten years. That's a huge amount that our kids and grandkids will be struggling with for years to come.

Peter, I'd hoped both parties could work together to vote for a bill that's a good deal for Indiana. But Mitch McConnell and his lobbyist pals simply wanted a giveaway for their donors that they could ram through with the minimum number of Republican votes. They'll try to sweep aside anyone who stood in their way.

I need your help today by contributing what you can to my campaign's Emergency Response Fund. Every dollar you give will go towards showing that our grassroots enthusiasm can fight back against the special interest cash about to flood our airwaves and pretend this partisan tax bill was something it's not.

**[Show the special interests you've got my back and contribute to our Emergency Response Fund today.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4eb1/768423100/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?refcode=20171202nd&amount=10") Every single donation you make counts.**

Thanks,
Joe

****[**CONTRIBUTE**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2510/7377f838/35e932b2/51fa4eaf/768423100/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?refcode=20171202nd&amount=25")****
---

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: in the middle of the night
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ | [**_There's an old saying: Don't get mad. Get even. Well, we need to get mad._**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b8e/70cdc2ba/46d497fa/3902643/2274158543/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/hold-republicans-accountable-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Theres+an+old+saying+Dont+get+mad+Get+ev&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1") [**_And then we need to GET EVEN and channel our anger to winning in 2018!_**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b8e/70cdc2ba/46d497fa/3902640/2274158543/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/hold-republicans-accountable-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+And+then+we+need+to+GET+EVEN+and+channel&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1")
---

What happened last night in the U.S. Senate was a dramatic display of abuse of power by the majority party.

Senate Republicans passed their tax plan to reward corporations and the very wealthy while punishing millions of hardworking Americans and exploding the deficit.

But that's not all: Republicans denied Senators an opportunity to even SEE what they were voting on. Many of the major provisions of the tax bill were added in the middle of the night and scribbled illegibly into the record.

Make no mistake - this tax bill will have a dramatic impact on the American people for years to come. But Republicans did not want the details of their plan to be discussed or even revealed before this critical vote. That's no way to legislate in a democracy.

[**It is now our job to make sure this is not the last word and see to it the American people are fully aware of this outrageous Republican tax plan. Sign here to pledge to fight back against this assault on hardworking Americans >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b8e/70cdc2ba/46d497fa/3902641/2274158543/VEsF/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/hold-republicans-accountable-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+It+is+now+our+job+to+make+sure+this+is+n&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1")

The examples of how this bill unfairly attacks everyday Americans, while favoring corporate interests, are numerous. In just one case, under this bill, working families across Maryland and elsewhere will now be double taxed on the money they send to state capitals to support schools and other state services; while corporations will still be permitted to deduct THEIR state taxes.

Republicans have made it perfectly clear who they represent -- they have chosen their wealthy donors and powerful corporations that can spend unlimited sums of money on politics thanks to the Citizens United ruling.

[**Please join me and pledge here to hold them accountable. We may have lost this vote, but the fight goes on, and we must never stop working to protect American families >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b8e/70cdc2ba/46d497fa/390265e/2274158543/VEsC/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/hold-republicans-accountable-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=4+-+Please+join+me+and+pledge+here+to+hold+t&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_120217_1")

We must hold them accountable in 2018,

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

name: Jason Yates, CEO of My Faith Votes
subject: Senate Passes Tax Reform - but it's not a done deal yet
body:
| | Latest information on tax reform
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171202_tr_s1&n=1)
| Hi Peter, Well, the Senate finally did it. Early this morning just before 2:00 am the Senate passed tax reform. It was a close vote just 51-49 with only one Republican voting no, Senator Bob Corker (R-Tenn.) It's been 31 years since Congress has passed comprehensive tax reform. The bill will now go to a conference where the House and the Senate bills will be compared and negotiations will take place before the bill reaches President Trump's desk. Thank you to those of you who took action and reminded your Senator of the great need to bring our tax code up to date into the 21st Century. Your voice was heard! Together, we sent thousands of messages to Washington. Let's continue to pray for those from the House and the Senate meeting in the Conference to hammer out the final details of the bill. Pray they would produce an outcome that is beneficial for all Americans. We'll keep you posted. So much is happening in Washington that affects the everyday lives of Americans. It's critical Christians stay alert and informed so that we can continue to seek the welfare of the cities in which we live. **United. We Stand.**

Jason Yates
CEO
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171202_tr_s1&n=2)
* * *

By partnering with local churches, pastors, and national faith leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics._

That starts with a better tax code that leaves more money in the pockets of the Hoosiers who leave for work in the dark and come home in the dark to provide for their families. It means making it easier for businesses to create more good-paying jobs in America, and rewarding them for keeping those jobs here at home.

**I 've tried to work with everyone I can during our current national conversation about our tax code. During the last few months, I've even spoken with the President multiple times about ideas that both parties agree would move us _forward_.** But Mitch McConnell and his special interest allies crafted this tax bill in a partisan process and rushed it to the floor with little debate. It's a handout to the richest Americans who don't need more help and the biggest corporations, many of which have shipped their money and jobs to other countries.

**I'm proud to have voted against it because we need to start over and _work together_ to write a tax bill that's focused on common sense reform for the American middle class families who need it most.**

The special interests who stand to gain from this bill have already spent millions of dollars to get me to betray my constituents. Now they'll spend tens of millions more trying to replace me with someone who puts them first, not Hoosiers. **President Trump even promised to come to Indiana and "campaign against [me] like you wouldn't believe" if I didn't go along with them here.**

**Can you chip in to my Emergency Response Fund to show Mitch McConnell we won't be stuck with a tax bill for special interest giveaways?** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [Express Donate $5](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c251e/7377f838/35e2275d/51fa4f26/538086952/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202.2&amount=5")
---
[Express Donate $10](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c251e/7377f838/35e2275d/51fa4f21/538086952/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202.2&amount=10")
[Express Donate $25](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c251e/7377f838/35e2275d/51fa4f20/538086952/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202.2&amount=25")
[Express Donate $50](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c251e/7377f838/35e2275d/51fa4f23/538086952/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?express_lane=true&refcode=20171202.2&amount=50")
[Donate another amount >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c251e/7377f838/35e2275d/51fa4f22/538086952/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?refcode=20171202.2")
Why is the McConnell tax bill wrong for Indiana? The list of reasons is long, but here are a few.
* Middle class Hoosier families deserve a tax cut, but this bill actually **RAISES taxes on a majority of families** earning less than $75,000 a year, including nearly one million Hoosier families. The cuts for most working families are temporary -- but the massive breaks for the biggest corporations are now permanent. Even worse, there's nothing here to encourage them to invest in American jobs, or penalize them for shipping jobs to foreign countries.

* This isn't just a bad tax bill -- it's a bad health care bill as well, especially for older Hoosiers. There's another attempt to dismantle Americans' health care system, one that would spike premiums and take away health insurance from enough Americans to equal Indiana's population twice over. And it **cuts Medicare by tens of billions of dollars at a minimum every single year.** Congress can't just give away older Hoosiers' hard-earned health care for another massive tax break to corporate America.

* Last, but not least, this bill is set to **add over _one trillion dollars_ \-- with a T! -- to our debt** over the next ten years. That's a huge amount that our kids and grandkids will be struggling with for years to come.

Peter, I'd hoped both parties could work together to vote for a bill that's a good deal for Indiana. But Mitch McConnell and his lobbyist pals simply wanted a giveaway for their donors that they could ram through with the minimum number of Republican votes. They'll try to sweep aside anyone who stood in their way.

I need your help today by contributing what you can to my campaign's Emergency Response Fund. Every dollar you give will go towards showing that our grassroots enthusiasm can fight back against the special interest cash about to flood our airwaves and pretend this partisan tax bill was something it's not.

**[Show the special interests you've got my back and contribute to our Emergency Response Fund today.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c251e/7377f838/35e2275d/51fa4f27/538086952/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-emergencymedia?refcode=20171202.2&amount=10") Every single donation you make counts.**

Thanks,
Joe
